top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer literally quotes 'ya enaṁ vetti hantāraṁ yaś cainaṁ manyate hatam' in the concrete scenario, names the symmetric joint negation, and structures the deprecation analog as a symmetric error pair (author guilt + engineer guilt). This is BG 2.19's defining symmetric structure.

BG 2.21 ('vedāvināśinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayam — kathaṁ sa puruṣaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam') has a similar agent-negation structure but adds the bhāva of one-who-knows. The analog here focuses on the joint negation, not on the knower's resulting non-action.
The verse is itself a near-verbatim quotation of Kaṭha Upaniṣad 1.2.19; the analog could in principle map to either. Distant since the project scope is BG verses.
